text: Consultant's review of the Subcontractor's submittals shall not relieve the Subcontractor of the obligations under the Contract Documents. The Consultant's review shall not constitute approval of  any  construction  means,  methods,  techniques,  sequences  or  procedures.  The  Consultant's review of submittals is not to be construed as a complete check nor relief from responsibility for any errors of any sort. The Consultant's approval of a specific item shall not indicate approval of an assembly of which the item is a component. Subcontractor acknowledges and agrees that the Consultant's review of the Subcontractor's design shall not relieve Subcontractor of obligations to provide  work  required  by  the  Contract  or  otherwise  reasonably  inferable  in  the  Contract Documents. The Subcontractor shall be solely responsible for accuracy, completeness, dimensions, details, quantities, applicable codes, and for coordination with other trades, whether shown on the Consultant's documents or not. The Consultant's design is a guide only and may not detail all the work that is involved in the completed project. Neither the review nor approval of Shop Drawings, Product Data, and Samples by the Contractor, or Consultant's shall be interpreted as  a  Change  Order  or  a  Change  Directive.  Further,  the  issuance  of  revised  or  supplemental drawings by the Contractor, or Consultant's shall not be interpreted as a Change Order or Change Directive.
